title: justicia albovelata
intro: Justicia albovelata, also known as white-flowered bugleweed, is a flowering plant species within the Acanthaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Justicia albovelata are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Justicia albovelata typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to treat various health conditions.
